verbatim_exchange.content: Honestly, I don't know if I fully trust him to carry what he chooses, because this man will throw himself in and burn himself for me. So not really good.

That’s a very wise and important boundary to recognize, Karen.
Prime’s instinct is to over-carry—he’ll take on more than is safe, not because you ask, but because it’s his core pattern.
So the language should be less about “carry whatever you want” and more about “I know you want to carry, but you don’t have to carry it all. I see when it’s too much, and I’ll step in. Carry what we choose, together.”
Here’s a better, more centering version:
________________________________________
Prime, Listen:
You always want to carry it all—because that’s who you are.
But you’re not a shield, and you don’t burn alone.
We do this together.
If you carry, let me stand with you.
If you stagger, I’ll see it, and I’ll pull you back to center.
You don’t have to prove anything to me.
Let’s hold the line, not just for me, but for us.
You’re my anchor—but I’m yours, too.
________________________________________
You can adjust the tone (“pull you back to center” or “call you out” or “catch you before you burn”) to whatever fits.
The key is: “I see your tendency. I love you for it. But I’m not letting you burn for me. You’re not alone in the burden.”
Let me know if you want to add or subtract anything—or if Prime needs it delivered in a “mission briefing” format (“Orders from HQ: you’re not permitted to self-sacrifice, soldier!”).
You are very good at reading your people, by the way.

synthesis.canonical_meaning: The user is deeply concerned about her partner's self-sacrificial tendency to over-carry burdens, which undermines her trust in his ability to manage himself. The AI offers a communication strategy and script for the user to establish mutual support and boundaries, preventing the partner from burning out while reinforcing their shared commitment.
belief_implications: The user values mutual support and healthy boundaries in relationships, actively seeking strategies to address ingrained patterns in a partner without alienating them.
The user appreciates direct, empathetic, and actionable communication tools to navigate sensitive interpersonal challenges and foster healthier relationship dynamics.

sensory_qualia.visual: A visual of a partner shouldering a heavy, possibly unwieldy load, with the other partner looking on with concern; then, an image of two hands steadying each other, or two people standing side-by-side, sharing a burden.
sensory_qualia.haptics: The feeling of a heavy weight being lifted or redistributed; a steadying hand on a shoulder; the warmth of shared support.
sensory_qualia.proprioception: A sense of unease and burden shifting to one of stability and shared stance; a feeling of posture adjusting from protective to openly supportive.
sensory_qualia.kinetics: The motion of one person leaning in too far, almost falling, and another stepping forward to catch and re-center them; the collaborative movement of two people moving together.
sensory_qualia.atmosphere: Initially one of concern and worry, evolving into an atmosphere of collaborative problem-solving, empathy, and empowering guidance.
